subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Join the Fluency Theater and help the cast read every word, phrase, and sentence clearly.
lesson.objective: I can read Grade 2 high-frequency words, phrases, and sentences accurately and smoothly.
lesson.concept_explanation: Fluent readers recognize common words automatically, read meaningful groups of words together, and use punctuation to make their voices match the text.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may skip small words, pause after every word, or ignore punctuation at the end of a sentence.
lesson.real_world_connection: Fluency helps with reading directions, stories, poems, and classroom messages.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read each word accurately, group words in phrases, and stop or change voice for punctuation.
lesson.mini_lesson: First, check every letter in an unfamiliar word. Next, group words that make one idea. Then reread the whole sentence, using the punctuation to guide your pauses and expression.

worked_examples.text: Watch: The word because should be recognized quickly without sounding out every letter.
Demo: Read around the tree as one smooth phrase, not three choppy words.
Demo: A question mark in Can you help me? tells the reader to use a question voice.

hint_ladder: Look at every word, including small function words.
Read the words together in a smooth phrase.
Let punctuation tell your voice when to stop or ask.

misconception_bank.sight_word_automaticity_gap: Learner needs repeated exposure before the word is automatic.
misconception_bank.phrase_chunking_error: Learner reads word by word instead of in a meaningful phrase.
misconception_bank.skips_function_words: Learner skips small words that carry sentence meaning.
misconception_bank.punctuation_ignored: Learner reads through punctuation without changing pace or stopping.

game_theme.mission_intro: Practice word cards, phrase lights, and sentence signs before showtime.

level_banks.label: Level 1: Grade 2 Sight Word Set A

questions.prompt: The class stayed inside ___ it was raining.
hints: Look closely at the word in the rainy-day recess item.
Read each choice and use the sentence meaning, word spelling, phrase grouping, or punctuation clue for because.
Check your choice by reading the complete word aloud; it should look right, make sense, and sound smooth.
questions.explanation: Because connects what happened with the reason it happened.
questions.feedback: Because connects what happened with the reason it happened.

question_audio.text: The class stayed inside ___ it was raining.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.explanation: A question mark ends a direct question and signals an asking voice.
questions.feedback: A question mark ends a direct question and signals an asking voice.

choices: calm statement: We won the relay.
asking: Did we win the relay?

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with punctuation that signals a calm stop.
questions.correct_answer: calm stop: The moon looks bright.
hints: Look closely at the punctuation in the bright moon item.
Read each choice and use the sentence meaning, word spelling, phrase grouping, or punctuation clue for period.
Check your choice by reading the complete punctuation aloud; it should look right, make sense, and sound smooth.
questions.explanation: A period ends this telling sentence and signals a full, calm stop.
questions.feedback: A period ends this telling sentence and signals a full, calm stop.
choices: calm stop: The moon looks bright.
asking voice: The moon looks bright?
excited voice: The moon looks bright!
unfinished pause: The moon looks bright,

story.text: Listen to this page. You are starting an English mission about Grade 2 Sight Words and Fluency. Look at the story and get ready to read carefully. Think about the sounds, words, sentences, or details that matter. Press Start Mission when you are ready.

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Grade 2 Sight Words and Fluency. Look for the reading or writing pattern on the page. Listen for word parts, important details, or sentence choices that help you decide. Remember to reread before you answer. Press Next to continue.
